title: audit — retention windows
description: 7 years default; 10 years max with audit_retention_extended entitlement. TenantErased MIGRATES to a compliance archive (never cascade-deletes — regulators may need proof of erasure years later). Exports themselves cold-archive after 90 days. Purge cadence: nightly per-tenant, honoring the active AuditRetentionPolicy.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation (7y default balances 'no longer than necessary' against regulator/audit requirements)
gdpr Art. 30 — records of processing must be maintained; audit is the record
hipaa §164.316(b)(2)(i) — 6 years minimum; we round up to 7
pci-dss Requirement 10.7 — audit trails retained minimum 1 year, immediately available past 3 months (our hot-tier 90d exceeds this)
iso 27001 A.12.4.1 — event logging retention policy
soc 2 CC7.1 — retain logs long enough for audit trail
ccpa §1798.185 — records of consumer requests (audit rows), 24 months minimum

hot.description: First 90 days in the primary Postgres `audits` table. Sub-100ms hot-read latency.

cold.description: Beyond 90d, ExportAuditsToArchiveJob writes tenant-scoped audit rows to a jsonl.gz file in the S3 archive bucket (`AUDIT_ARCHIVE_S3_BUCKET`) and prunes them from the hot table (Enterprise only — Small + Medium keep everything hot until final purge).

purge.trigger: created_at + retention_years < now() (per active AuditRetentionPolicy or config.audit.retention.default_years=7)
purge.action: hard delete from hot table + delete from cold archive + write meta-audit summary row

override.description: Enterprise tenants create an AuditRetentionPolicy row with retention_years up to 10 + an override_reason. Extension bumps the cutoff computation.

hot.description: Retained while active OR while deleted_at within a 1-year grace (so the compliance officer can audit the policy history).

purge.action: hard delete + write summary audit row via HasAuditable on the policy itself

hot.description: Export metadata rows (request timestamp, requester, row count, signed URL, S3 key) retained 90 days for compliance audit ('who exported what and when').

cold.description: The signed S3 URL expires at 24h (config.audit.export.signed_url_ttl_hours). The metadata row lives 90d past that for the compliance audit.

purge.trigger: created_at > 90 days AND signed_url has expired

retention.action: MIGRATE audit rows to a compliance archive (S3 bucket named for the erasure event + tenant ID). Do NOT cascade-delete — regulators may need proof of erasure years later. The retention window still applies to the migrated payload; PurgeExpiredAuditsJob purges the archive at the same cutoff.
retention.note: This is deliberately DIFFERENT from analytics/marketing/activity's hard-delete cascade. Audit is regulator evidence; erasure of a subject does not remove the record of the erasure itself + the mutations that led up to it.

PurgeExpiredAuditsJob.purpose: Nightly per-tenant purge. Honors active AuditRetentionPolicy retention_years (default 7). Writes summary meta-audit ('audit.purged { tenant_id, cutoff_at, row_count }') so the trail records itself being purged.

ExpireRetentionOverridesJob.purpose: Nightly — checks every AuditRetentionPolicy where override_ends_at <= now() AND retention_years > default_years. Resets retention_years to default_years + fires AuditRetentionExtensionExpired. Sends AuditRetentionExtensionExpiringSoonNotification 30 days ahead of expiry.

ExportAuditsToArchiveJob.purpose: Weekly Sunday 05:00 UTC. Enterprise tenants — writes 90d+ audit rows to jsonl.gz in S3 archive bucket. Prunes hot table after successful upload verification.
